candidates.title: What some immigrants could lose if Alberta votes yes to Danielle Smith's questions
candidates.description: To many voters, the various questions on the Oct. 19 ballots that cover social services for newcomers will appear confusing and contradictory.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Sask. man's death ruled accidental, despite initial RCMP response deemed 'wholly unacceptable'
candidates.description: When RCMP got a call about an unmanned boat going in circles on Weyakwin Lake, officers didn't make the connection that it could mean a person was missing. Matthew Hamm's body was found in the lake the following spring. The RCMP's civilian watchdog says the officers' initial response was "wholly unacceptable," and Hamm's parents still have questions.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta First Nations intend to 'rock the vote' against separatism
candidates.description: First Nations leaders in Alberta are turning to a campaign that saw record on-reserve voter turnout in an effort to tip the scales against the separatist movement during October's provincial referendum.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Is there an alternative to killing nuisance black bears?
candidates.description: It’s a common debate in communities next to bear habitat — should you call the B.C. Conservation Officer Service to report a bear looking for sources of human food like garbage or kids’ lunch boxes? Some encourage others not to report such incidents because of a belief that it amounts to a death sentence for the bear.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: What the 1976 Montreal Games represent to those who boycotted it, 50 years later
candidates.description: A young Nigerian woman lands in Montreal, full of excitement and optimism for her first Olympic Games. A Tanzanian track star never leaves home. On the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Games, both athletes reflect on the boycott that demanded they put aside their dreams for a greater cause.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: N.S. says Eastern Shore wildfire now being held, residents can return home
candidates.description: A wildfire on Nova Scotia's Eastern Shore is now listed as being held, but ground crews will continue the containment efforts throughout the day. Residents evacuated on Thursday are now allowed to return to their homes.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alcohol costs P.E.I. more than it brings in, new provincial strategy says
candidates.description: Prince Edward Island has released a new provincial alcohol strategy aimed at reducing illness and harms linked to drinking, and experts say it puts the province ahead of much of the country.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Essential workers evacuate Wrigley, N.W.T., as fire nears
candidates.description: Chief of the Pehdzéh Kı̨ First Nation says the fire moved towards the community overnight, and crews have been forced to abandon their line for their own safety. Air tankers put down fire retardant, but the fire crossed that line too. All remaining workers are to make their way to Fort Smith.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: B.C. wildfire tally surges past 100 as barrage of lightning strikes transforms fire season in a flash
candidates.description: On Wednesday morning the province's firefighters faced only about 20 blazes in what had been a relatively quiet season so far, but as of Saturday morning there were 113 fires burning, including about 100 started since Friday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Blue Jays unveil Joe Carter statue outside Rogers Centre honouring World Series championships
candidates.description: A statue honouring the 1992 and 1993 World Series champion Toronto Blue Jays has been unveiled outside of Rogers Centre.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Conditions for wildland firefighters 'extremely challenging' in northwestern Ontario: union
candidates.description: The union representing Ontario's wildland firefighters says conditions for crews battling forest fires in northwestern Ontario are "extremely challenging."
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: AFN calls on Ottawa, provinces to protect treaty rights in wake of Alberta separatism
candidates.description: First Nations chiefs from across Canada are calling on the federal and provincial governments to protect treaty rights from, what they say are, threats posed by Alberta separatism.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Over time, more frequent floods and wildfires are threatening Manitoba's fiscal resilience
candidates.description: The combined cost of wildfires and flooding over the past two years will have a serious impact on the provincial budget in the short term. How Manitoba responds to these disasters on a longer-term basis will be even more significant.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: From deficit to surplus? Alberta's bleak budget outlook 'changed dramatically' as oil prices rise
candidates.description: Alberta's financial outlook has taken a dramatic turn after the province previously forecasted a $9.4-billion shortfall. Surging oil prices could mean the province finishes the fiscal year in the black.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Air quality warning lifted across Greater Toronto Area: Environment Canada
candidates.description: Environment Canada lifted an air quality warning across the Greater Toronto Area on Sunday, after heavy smoke blanketed the region over the past week.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Family of 6 flees Boston Bar fires with 15 pets in tow
candidates.description: When Brittany Alenius, her husband and their four children were told to evacuate, their prized possessions were packed up and ready to go. But the trickiest part was making space in the car for three dogs, six cats, two snakes, an iguana and three birds.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Boden Umpherville's family continues search for answers at coroner's inquest
candidates.description: Family and friends have always disputed the investigation that cleared Prince Albert police of any wrongdoing. A public inquest starting Monday will determine the facts of the 40-year-old's death in 2023.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Judge declares mistrial in businessman Frank Stronach's sexual assault conviction
candidates.description: A lawyer for billionaire businessman Frank Stronach says a judge has declared a mistrial on his sexual assault conviction in a surprise ruling ahead of his scheduled September sentencing hearing. The Ministry of the Attorney General confirmed the mistrial but did not provide details.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. renews strikes on Iran after 2 American military members killed
candidates.description: The U.S. said it had completed an eighth straight night of attacks on Iran after earlier announcing that two U.S. military personnel were killed in Jordan, while U.S. allies in the ‌region reported more Iranian attacks on Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: How modern warfare is reshaping Ukraine’s natural environment
candidates.description: From Chornobyl's endangered wild horses to bird nests made of drone cables, modern warfare is reshaping fragile ecosystems along Ukraine’s front lines.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada to temporarily bar entry by foreign nationals recently in Congo due to Ebola risk
candidates.description: As part of new, temporary border measures ‌to combat the spread ​of Ebola, the ​Canadian government on Sunday said ​it would deny entry to ‌foreign nationals who had been to the Democratic Republic of the Congo ‌within the past 21 days.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Is this the most controversial World Cup yet?
candidates.description: After more than 100 matches across Canada, Mexico and the United States, the FIFA World Cup 2026 is about to come to an end, cementing its legacy with highs, lows — and seemingly endless controversies. So, is this the most controversial World Cup? We talk to experts to get a sense of how this year's decisions have affected the tournament.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Russian ballistic missile attack on Kyiv kills 1, wounds 16
candidates.description: Russia battered the Ukrainian capital of Kyiv overnight ‌with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war that began in February 2022.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump threatens increased tariffs for wildfire smoke that has 'unnecessarily invaded' U.S.
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is adding his voice to the chorus of Republican politicians who are blaming Canada for wildfire smoke drifting across the border.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran supreme leader threatens to teach U.S. 'unforgettable lessons' if attacks continue
candidates.description: Iran's supreme leader warned of "unforgettable lessons" Saturday if the United States keeps attacking the Islamic Republic and called President Donald Trump's signature "worthless and invalid." The comments came hours after a negotiator said Tehran was suspending its commitments to the interim deal signed about a month ago.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: This World Cup photographer used a 96-year-old camera 'in a world where everything is instant'
candidates.description: Fareed Kotb, a sports photographer from Cairo, garnered international attention when he was spotted snapping World Cup games using a 1930 large-format film camera.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Saka's hat trick lifts England past Mbappé, France 6-4 in wild World Cup 3rd-place game
candidates.description: Bukayo Saka scored three goals to lead England to a 6-4 win over France for third place in the World Cup, while Kylian Mbappe surpassed Lionel Messi's career World Cup scoring record and took the lead in the Golden Boot race with a pair of second-half goals on Saturday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Mexico not looking to cut side deal with U.S. and leave Canada out, foreign secretary says
candidates.description: Mexico's foreign secretary says his country isn't looking to cut a deal with the U.S. if it means abandoning the trilateral trade pact with Canada.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Why travel insurance may not help you if there's a WestJet strike
candidates.description: WestJet travellers hoping to buy protection in case their travel plans are interrupted or delayed by a contract dispute may be out of luck, as travel insurance experts say policies purchased after potential strike dates are announced won't cover future work stoppages.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Narcissistic leaders more likely to oppose remote work, new research suggests
candidates.description: As more employees are being ordered back into the office full time, new research suggests the rationale may be more about bosses' desire for power and status than productivity.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Government says it's investigating CN's actions after rail crew surrounded by wildfire
candidates.description: Transport Canada said it's investigating to determine if CN failed to comply with rules under the Railway Safety Act, or if health and safety violations occurred. The union representing rail workers has also criticized the rail company for the incident, saying it never should have happened.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
candidates.description: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
candidates.description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
candidates.description: Five people are killed and at least 35 injured as Ukraine's two largest cities are targeted, as Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Health officials identify source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak
candidates.description: The US Food and Drug Administration says iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell and Walmart locations was the source of cyclosporiasis infections.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90
candidates.description: His son, actor Jason Donovan, posted to social media that his father died on Saturday night peacefully in Melbourne, Australia
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
candidates.description: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
candidates.description: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: White House defends Argentina team over Falklands banner
candidates.description: The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Messi on the brink of history - will it be his last World Cup game?
candidates.description: Argentina captain Lionel Messi will play his 34th World Cup match in Sunday's final, but will it be his last appearance?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
candidates.description: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
candidates.description: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Why the World Cup half-time show may be a taste of things to come
candidates.description: The 2026 World Cup's spectacle is creating debate about the place of music in sport - and how it could infiltrate other tournaments.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
candidates.description: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: How these twins' rare growth disorder could provide the key to preventing cancer
candidates.description: Researchers have found that people with Laron syndrome have a lower incidence of cancer than the wider population.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Brad Pitt's children are dropping their dad's surname - here's why I did the same
candidates.description: Hannah had used her mother's maiden name on her social media for years before she legally made the change.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Helicopters dump water as huge Norway fire destroys homes
candidates.description: The blaze has destroyed more than 100 homes in Drammen and has spread to a nearby forest, authorities say.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Is Burnham promising a new dawn for North Sea oil and gas?
candidates.description: The incoming PM is expected to announce fresh support for the North Sea from day one of his premiership.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Toronto, New York and Washington top list of most polluted cities as wildfires burn
candidates.description: Canadian officials say that fire activity in Ontario is beginning to ease, with air quality expected to improve in US cities too.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Second half of summer to bring chances for rain but heatwave threat persists
candidates.description: Forecasts suggest the weather could turn more changeable during late July and August but the risk of heatwaves remains.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: EU proposes slowing down cuts to carbon emissions for businesses
candidates.description: Under the proposals, the EU plans to relax its emissions trading system to give companies more time to reduce their carbon output.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: How new bird show hopes to help endangered flyers
candidates.description: Endangered birds are the stars of one of five new shows at Kynren as part of conservation plans.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Britain 'set for bumper year' for butterflies
candidates.description: A hot and dry spring means a greater variety and more butterflies across the UK, a charity says.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
candidates.description: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
candidates.description: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
candidates.description: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
candidates.description: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Florida on charges of rape and sex trafficking
candidates.description: Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in Miami after British authorities issued 38 new charges against them, and said they are "seeking extradition" for the two men.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
candidates.description: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. launches new airstrikes to 'punish' Iran for troop deaths
candidates.description: Two U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
candidates.description: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: 8 killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
candidates.description: Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The U.S. and Iran blow past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
candidates.description: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The 2000s called. They want their digital camera back
candidates.description: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong
candidates.description: The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: The new $1 Trump coin doesn't just buck norms. Experts say it also breaks laws
candidates.description: President Trump's face will appear on a new commemorative coin honoring the nation's 250th birthday. It's one of the many unusual places his likeness has popped up this year.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Violence repeatedly erupts in dementia care despite warnings, inspections show
candidates.description: Nursing homes and assisted living facilities can be fertile ground for violence between residents with dementia, including fatal assaults. Court records and inspecitions how safeguards fall short.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: ICE shared Medicaid data it wasn't supposed to have with Palantir
candidates.description: The revelations came out in a federal court case brought by Democratic states challenging ICE's access to Medicaid data to aid in deportation efforts.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Cyclospora outbreak: FDA says do not eat shredded lettuce from Taco Bell in 5 states
candidates.description: Shredded lettuce served at Taco Bell in Michigan, Ohio, Kentucky, West Virginia and Indiana is linked to cyclospora outbreak. Investigation continues
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued July 19 at 12:19PM CDT until July 20 at 10:00PM CDT by NWS Omaha/Valley NE
candidates.description: * WHAT...Heat index values up to 105 today. Heat index values up to 105 to 110 on Monday. * WHERE...Antelope, Cedar, Knox, and Pierce Counties. * WHEN...Until 10 PM CDT Monday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Overnight heat index values between 75 to 80 will provide little relief.

candidates.description: * WHAT...Heat index values up to 105 to 110. * WHERE...Portions of southwest and west central Iowa and east central, northeast, and southeast Nebraska. * WHEN...From 10 AM to 10 PM CDT Monday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Overnight heat index values between 75 to 80 will provide little relief.

candidates.description: SVRBMX The National Weather Service in Birmingham has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Southwestern Walker County in central Alabama... East central Fayette County in west central Alabama... * Until 100 PM CDT. * At 1218 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located near Oakman, or 12 miles south of Carbon Hill, moving southeast at 10 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Parrish, Oakman, Gorgas Steam Plant, Gorgas, Goodsprings, The Wye, Tutwiler, Corona, and West Corona.

For the second Heat Advisory, heat index values up to 108 expected. * WHERE...George, Greene, Perry, Stone, and Wayne Counties. * WHEN...For the first Heat Advisory, until 7 PM CDT this evening. For the second Heat Advisory, from 10 AM Monday to 7 PM CDT Tuesday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses.

For the second Heat Advisory, from 10 AM Monday to 7 PM CDT Tuesday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses.

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 19 at 1:15PM EDT by NWS Peachtree City GA
candidates.description: At 114 PM EDT, a strong thunderstorm was over Pisgah, or 11 miles south of Blue Ridge. This storm was nearly stationary. HAZARD...Up to 40 mph wind, frequent cloud to ground lightning and heavy rain. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect minor damage to tree limbs and blowing around of light, unsecured objects. Heavy rain may cause temporary street flooding especially in poor drainage areas. Locations impacted include... Dial, Springer Mountain Shelter, Len Foote Hike Inn, Camp Merrill, Pisgah, Black Gap Shelter, Stover Creek Shelter, Hawk Mountain Shelter, and Roy.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Air Quality Alert issued July 19 at 12:13PM CDT by NWS Quad Cities IA IL
candidates.description: The Illinois Environmental Protection Agency has issued an Areawide Air Pollution Action Day for Monday July 20th for portions of Illinois. This is in addition to the ongoing Areawide Air Pollution Action Day in effect today July 19th for the same areas. An Areawide Air Pollution Action Day is declared when weather conditions are such that widespread ozone and or particulate levels are forecast to be at OR ABOVE the Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups (USG) category in all or nearly all Illinois AQI forecast sectors. Areas both inside and outside of the Action Day coverage area are likely to see smoke/PM2.5 to a varying degree and duration today and Monday with smoke likely decreasing from southwest to northeast through the day Monday. Those with air quality concerns or anyone in Illinois with outdoor plans are encouraged to check current NowCast readings or sign up for EnviroFlash alerts on AirNow. gov. Before going outside, checking the NowCast AQI will help identify periods of both poor and cleaner air quality regardless of the level of the daily 24-hour AQI forecast. AQI Category Guidance Unhealthy for Sensitive Groups (USG) - Members of sensitive groups may experience health effects. The general public is less likely to be affected. Sensitive groups: Make outdoor activities shorter and less intense. Its OK to be active outdoors, but take more breaks. Watch for symptoms such as coughing or shortness of breath. People with asthma: Follow your asthma action plan and keep quick relief medicine handy. People with heart disease: Symptoms such as palpitations, shortness of breath, or unusual fatigue may indicate a serious problem. If you have any of these, contact your health care provider. Unhealthy - Sensitive groups: Avoid long or intense outdoor activities. Consider rescheduling or moving activities indoors. Everyone else: Reduce long or intense activities. Take more breaks during outdoor activities. Air quality forecasts and current air quality can be found at www.airnow.gov Media Contact... Illinois EPA
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Heat Advisory issued July 19 at 12:11PM CDT until July 19 at 6:00PM CDT by NWS New Orleans LA
candidates.description: * WHAT...For the first Heat Advisory, heat index values up to 109. For the second Heat Advisory, heat index values up to 110 expected. * WHERE...Portions of southeast Louisiana and southern Mississippi. * WHEN...For the first Heat Advisory, until 6 PM CDT this evening. For the second Heat Advisory, from noon to 7 PM CDT Monday. * IMPACTS...Hot temperatures and high humidity may cause heat illnesses.

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 19 at 1:08PM EDT by NWS Raleigh NC
candidates.description: At 108 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking strong thunderstorms along a line extending from 9 miles west of Norlina to 6 miles east of Warrenton. Movement was east at 25 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph, torrential rain, and pea size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation is possible. Locations impacted include... Henderson, Warrenton, Norlina, Littleton, Middleburg, Macon, Steele Creek Marina & Campground, Kerr Lake, Lake Gaston, and Wise. This includes Interstate 85 between Mile Markers 215 and 233.

candidates.title: M 5.5 - 2 km WSW of Sicaya, Peru
candidates.description: The United States Geological Survey reported an earthquake of magnitude 5.5. The event occurred near 2 km WSW of Sicaya, Peru. Official monitoring data may be updated as scientists review additional measurements.
candidates.author: U.S. Geological Survey
